content.problemStatement:
In a school, there are CCC clubs (numbered 111 through CCC) that students can participate in. Each student can be in zero, one or multiple clubs.

Tomorrow, there is a school assembly and NNN students (numbered 111 through NNN) will be standing in a line. Each student should wear one of MMM single-colour shirts provided by the school. The only restriction is that for each club, students in this club must wear shirts with the same colour (possibly the same colour for multiple clubs).

Students in the same clubs try to stand near each other. Formally, for each club iii, you are given xix_ixi‚Äã segments ‚Äî let's denote them by [l1,r1],[l2,r2],‚Ä¶,[lxi,rxi][l_1,r_1],[l_2,r_2], \ldots, [l_{x_i},r_{x_i}][l1‚Äã,r1‚Äã],[l2‚Äã,r2‚Äã],‚Ä¶,[lxi‚Äã‚Äã,rxi‚Äã‚Äã], where 1‚â§l1‚â§r1<l2‚â§r2<‚Ä¶<lxi‚â§rxi‚â§N1 \le l_1 \le r_1 \lt l_2 \le r_2 \lt \ldots \lt l_{x_i} \le r_{x_i} \le N1‚â§l1‚Äã‚â§r1‚Äã<l2‚Äã‚â§r2‚Äã<‚Ä¶<lxi‚Äã‚Äã‚â§rxi‚Äã‚Äã‚â§N ‚Äî such for each valid jjj, the students lj,lj+1,‚Ä¶,rjl_j, l_j+1, \ldots, r_jlj‚Äã,lj‚Äã+1,‚Ä¶,rj‚Äã belong to this club.

Determine the number of ways in which the students can choose the colours of their shirts. Two ways to choose colours are considered different if there is at least one student wearing a shirt with a different colour. Since this number can be very large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers CCC, NNN and MMM.
Then, 2C2C2C lines follow. For each valid iii, the (2i‚àí1)(2i-1)(2i‚àí1)-th of these lines contains a single integer xix_ixi‚Äã and the 2i2i2i-th line contains 2‚ãÖxi2 \cdot x_i2‚ãÖxi‚Äã space-separated integers l1,r1,l2,r2,‚Ä¶,lxi,rxil_1,r_1,l_2,r_2, \ldots, l_{x_i},r_{x_i}l1‚Äã,r1‚Äã,l2‚Äã,r2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,lxi‚Äã‚Äã,rxi‚Äã‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of ways to choose colours modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Students 555 and 666 are in both clubs, so these two clubs must wear shirts with the same colour. Student 333 does not participate in any club and can wear a shirt with any colour out of the three available options.
